news.title: Goa's town planning department notifies 14 proposals for zone change under 39A
news.para: The TCP department has proposed converting over 11,900 sqm of green cover and agricultural land into settlement zones across four talukas. These proposals, which include significant conversions in Bardez, Ponda, Quepem, and Salcete, are now open for public objections. Final approvals for some plots are contingent on clearances from the forest department and other authorities.

news.title: Noida: Residents of La Residentia renew protest over maintenance woes
news.para: Residents of La Residentia Society in Greater Noida West are protesting for the second day over alleged builder apathy and unresolved maintenance issues.  Families are demanding an urgent meeting to address problems like irregular water supply, faulty lifts, and poor upkeep. A peaceful sit-in and a 10-day camp are planned to intensify the agitation.

news.title: Patna civic body recovers ₹1-crore property tax in special drive
news.para: In a swift enforcement initiative, Patna Municipal Corporation has successfully collected close to Rs 1 crore in property taxes in just 48 hours, primarily aimed at big defaulters. An agile team is on the ground, securing payments with the threat of freezing bank accounts and seizing properties for noncompliance.

news.title: Haryana revenue department launches Bhu-Mitra chatbot for land, revenue services
news.para: Haryana's revenue department has launched Bhu-Mitra, a WhatsApp chatbot offering 24/7 land and revenue services in Hindi and English. This initiative aims to enhance accessibility and efficiency by allowing citizens to access services like land records, mutation, and deed registration from their smartphones, eliminating the need for physical visits to government offices.

news.title: Ludhiana development body issues notices to 43 property owners over illegal construction
news.para: Greater Ludhiana Area Development Authority is taking action against illegal construction. Notices have been issued to 43 property owners in Shaheed Sukhdev Singh Nagar. This follows complaints about commercial buildings in residential areas. Glada is now canceling building plans and plans property seals and demolitions. Residents are advised to check official websites before buying property.

news.title: Lucknow development body to use drones to identify illegal buildings
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) is employing drones and satellite imagery to detect buildings and commercial complexes constructed without approved plans or in violation of bylaws. This enforcement drive, focusing initially on major roads, aims to identify unauthorized land use changes and deviations from sanctioned layouts. Regular drone surveys will ensure ongoing monitoring and early detection of new violations.

news.title: Jammu & Kashmir achieves 97% target under PMAY-G, 3.23 lakh houses built
news.para: Jammu and Kashmir has successfully completed over 3 lakh houses under Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Gramin, achieving 97% of its target. Officials are now urged to expedite work on the remaining 11,340 houses, with a strict deadline of April 15 for all pending constructions before the new phase commences.

news.title: Thane civic body grants 90% penalty relief to property tax defaulters
news.para: Thane Municipal Corporation has announced a 90% penalty waiver on property tax arrears to boost recovery. Residential property owners will now pay only 10% of the penalty, aiming to recover pending dues before the fiscal year ends. This move, however, has drawn criticism from timely taxpayers.

news.title: No new affordable-housing blueprints on Ludhiana Improvement Trust table for 20 years.
news.para: Ludhiana Improvement Trust has not launched any new affordable housing schemes for residents in twenty years. The agency's focus has shifted to minor development works. The Atal Apartment project is nearing completion in 2026. This lack of government projects forces residents to choose expensive private developers or illegal colonies. Past expansion attempts have failed.

news.title: Goa: Consumer forum tells developer to repair seepage in flat
news.para: A property developer has been ordered by the North Goa District Consumer Dispute Redressal Commission to repair water seepage in a flat within three months. The developer must also pay Rs 25,000 for damages caused by leakage. The commission found the builder responsible for addressing structural cracks and defective waterproofing.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh stamps department opens campaign to boost revenue collection
news.para: The Stamp and Registration Department is actively working to increase revenue before the financial year ends. They are prioritizing the registration of unregistered properties from various authorities and institutions. This initiative aims to collect significant revenue through timely stamp duty payments on ongoing projects.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh RERA chief tells builders to mandatorily register their projects
news.para: AP RERA chairman A Siva Reddy has mandated registration for housing projects exceeding 500 square metres or eight apartments.  He highlighted the RERA Act's role in promoting the construction sector and protecting homebuyers, with penalties for non-compliance.  An opportunity for unregistered projects to register with a penalty subsidy is available until March 31.

news.title: Visakhapatnam: VMRDA begins work on ‘Vizag Bay City’ coastal project
news.para: Visakhapatnam is set to transform its coastline into a vibrant 'Vizag Bay City,' a 40-sq-km integrated hub inspired by global models. The ambitious plan focuses on boosting coastal tourism, developing a global business ecosystem, and creating a premium residential environment. This initiative aims to attract millions of visitors and professionals, revitalizing the region.

news.title: Telangana government yet to set up municipal building tribunal despite HC directive in 2022
news.para: Despite a decade-old provision and a four-year-old assurance to the Telangana High Court, the state government has failed to establish the Municipal Building Tribunal (MBT). This delay exacerbates the mushrooming of unauthorized structures, leaving thousands of cases pending in courts. The tribunal, intended to streamline building-related disputes, remains elusive, impacting effective governance and legal recourse.

news.title: Maharashtra government to set up new IT park near proposed Purandar airport
news.para: Maharashtra government announced a new IT park near the proposed international airport in Purandar. The move aims to attract IT companies by offering better infrastructure, potentially creating one lakh jobs. Experts urged the government to learn from Hinjewadi's past mistakes and ensure world-class amenities.

news.title: Kochi: After years-long wait, Bliss City project to become reality
news.para: Kochi Metro Rail Ltd's ambitious Bliss City project in Kakkanad is moving forward with the leasing of its first land parcels. This 31.43-acre mixed-use development, a collaboration with NBCC, will feature commercial, shopping, and entertainment facilities, including a proposed theme park and I-Max theatre, aiming to become a premier business and leisure destination.

news.title: Developers, RWAs in Gurugram told to submit action-taken reports on complaints on structural safety
news.para: Gurugram's district administration will convene a meeting on March 17 with developers and RWAs to address non-implementation of structural audit repair recommendations. Residents have alleged that crucial repair and strengthening measures for several group housing colonies have not been properly executed. The meeting aims to review compliance status and identify pending works to ensure building safety.

news.title: Telangana RERA imposes ₹4.7 crore penalty on Bharathi Builders for pre-lauch sales
news.para: Telangana RERA has imposed a record Rs 4.7 crore penalty on Bharathi Builders for illegal pre-launch sales of its 'Bharathi Lake View Apartments' project. The builder collected substantial amounts from buyers without RERA registration and failed to commence construction or deliver the project within the promised timeline. Aggrieved homebuyers will receive full refunds with interest.
